messages.content: isn't it wild how a coffee that was like 2 bucks when i was a teenager is basically 6 now

Yeah, it really does hit different when you think back to those prices! Though some of that is just inflation doing its thing over the years - the dollar just buys less than it used to.

But coffee specifically has also had some other stuff going on - like the whole "craft coffee" culture took off and kind of pulled prices up across the board, even for pretty basic drinks. Plus supply chain stuff, labor costs, rent for those shops...

Do you find yourself still buying it regularly at those prices, or has it made you cut back? A lot of people I talk to have kind of shifted to making more at home because of it.
i was telling my coworker, feels like everything just quietly crept up and we all kinda accepted it

That's such a good way to put it - "quietly crept up." It really does happen gradually enough that there's no single moment where you go "okay THIS is too much," you just kind of... adjust and keep going.

It's almost like the frog in slowly heating water thing. Each individual price bump feels small enough to absorb, but then you look back and realize your whole cost of living is just fundamentally different than it was.

And I think there's something social about it too - when everyone around you is paying the same prices, it normalizes it pretty fast. Like if your whole office is still grabbing that $6 coffee, it feels weird to be the one making a big deal about it.

Did your coworker feel the same way, or were they more like "eh that's just how it is"?
makes me a little nostalgic for the days when a 20 in your pocket felt like real money lol

Ha, right?! A $20 used to feel like you were genuinely set for the day - grab food, drinks, maybe still have change left over. Now it's like... that's basically one decent lunch and a coffee and you're done.

There's something kind of bittersweet about that nostalgia though. Like part of it is probably just being younger and having simpler needs, but part of it is genuinely real - that money just had more reach back then.

I feel like a lot of people have that moment where they hand over a $20 for something totally ordinary and think "wait, when did THIS become a $20 thing?"

What's the one that gets you the most - like is there a specific thing where the price always catches you off guard still?
anyway not complaining really, just one of those shower thoughts about how prices change over time

Haha yeah totally, it's one of those things that's just kind of interesting to think about rather than actually stressing over. Like your brain just randomly connects the dots sometimes and goes "huh, wild."

Shower thoughts are honestly some of the best ones - no real agenda, just your mind wandering and noticing stuff about the world. 

Hope the rest of your Tuesday is good! You heading back into work stuff now or taking a lunch break?
